description: A lightweight iOS mood tracker with widget-first check-ins, trend visualization, and data export — capturing moods in under 5 seconds while providing meaningful insights over time.

tasks.title: Project Setup & iOS 17+ Configuration
tasks.description: Configure the project for iOS 17+ deployment, rename TemplateApp to MoodFlicker2, update bundle identifier, and ensure all build settings align with iOS 17+ requirements. Update project.yml with new app identity and regenerate Xcode project.
acceptanceCriteria: Deployment target is set to iOS 17.0 or higher in project.yml
App target is renamed from TemplateApp to MoodFlicker2
Bundle identifier updated to com.cankocakulak.moodflicker2
Project generates successfully via Scripts/generate_project.sh
App builds without errors after renaming

scenarios: Project builds successfully with new identity
validation.notes: This is foundational - all other tasks depend on proper project setup. Must complete before any feature work.

tasks.notes: Implementation Boundary: Project configuration and identity only. PRD refs: PR-009. Bootstrap handoff: Use working branch codex/mood-flicker2-v1.

tasks.title: SwiftData Model & Local Storage Layer
tasks.description: Create the MoodEntry SwiftData model with mood value (Int 1-5), timestamp (Date), and optional tag (String). Implement CRUD operations, data persistence layer, and query capabilities. Replace existing LocalStore placeholder.
acceptanceCriteria: MoodEntry model defined with @Model macro
Model includes: id (UUID), moodValue (Int), timestamp (Date), tag (String?, optional)
Data persists across app launches using SwiftData
Query by date range returns results in chronological order
Works without internet connection

miniScenarios: App launches without SwiftData migration errors
Can create and save a MoodEntry instance
scenarios: Data persists after force-quit and relaunch
Query returns entries in correct order
validation.notes: Critical foundation for all data-dependent features. Model design must support future iCloud sync without migration.

tasks.notes: Implementation Boundary: Data model and persistence layer only. No UI. PRD refs: PR-008.

tasks.title: Widget Extension & Quick Check-In
tasks.description: Create WidgetKit extension with interactive widget showing 5 emoji buttons (😔 😟 😐 🙂 😄). Implement App Intent for handling mood selection, widget refresh after check-in, haptic feedback, and relative time display ('Just now', '5m ago').
acceptanceCriteria: Widget extension added to project with proper target configuration
Widget displays 5 emoji buttons horizontally
Tapping emoji saves mood entry via App Intent
Widget shows checkmark confirmation (0.5s) after tap
Widget displays 'Last check-in: Just now' with relative time updates
Haptic feedback triggered on widget interaction

miniScenarios: Widget preview renders in Xcode canvas
scenarios: Widget tap creates mood entry visible in app
Widget updates timestamp after check-in
Widget shows checkmark confirmation for 0.5s after tap
validation.notes: Requires iOS 17+ for interactive widgets. Widget refresh may have system-imposed rate limits.

tasks.notes: COMPLETED: Widget checkmark confirmation (0.5s) implemented. MoodEntryTimelineEntry now has showingConfirmation flag and confirmationMoodValue. LogMoodIntent sets confirmation state before saving and schedules refresh to clear after 0.5s. ConfirmationView shows large emoji with green circle and 'Logged!' text.

tasks.title: Implement haptic feedback for widget interactions
tasks.description: Widget App Intents cannot directly trigger haptics. Implement haptic feedback coordination between widget and main app. Options: (1) Main app detects new widget entry and triggers haptic on next launch, (2) Use CoreHaptics if available, (3) Document as known WidgetKit limitation.
acceptanceCriteria: Haptic feedback triggered when user logs mood via widget
Light impact feedback (UIImpactFeedbackStyle.light) on successful mood log
Feedback works when app is in foreground or background

scenarios: Haptic feedback triggers on widget mood log
validation.notes: WidgetKit limitation: App Intents cannot directly trigger haptics. Requires main app coordination.

tasks.notes: COMPLETED: Implemented HapticManager with main app coordination. Widget sets 'widgetHapticPending' flag in UserDefaults when mood is logged. Main app checks this flag on scenePhase .active and triggers success haptic. Supports both CoreHaptics (richer) and UINotificationFeedbackGenerator (fallback).

tasks.title: Today View UI
tasks.description: Create the Today tab showing today's mood entries sorted by time (newest first). Include entry display with emoji, time, and optional tag, pull-to-refresh, swipe-to-delete, and friendly empty state with widget guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: Today view displays list of today's entries sorted by time (newest first)
Each entry shows emoji, exact time (e.g., '2:30 PM'), and optional tag
Empty state shows illustration and 'Add the MoodFlicker2 widget' guidance
Pull-to-refresh updates the list
Swipe left reveals delete action with confirmation

miniScenarios: Empty state displays when no entries
scenarios: Entries display with correct emoji and time
Delete action removes entry and updates list
validation.notes: Empty state illustration should be simple, calm, and on-brand.

tasks.notes: Implementation Boundary: Today tab UI only. Does NOT include charts or insights. PRD refs: PR-004.

tasks.title: Context Tags Feature
tasks.description: Implement optional context tags for mood entries. Add 'Add Context' button to entries, tag selector with 5 preset options (Work, Sleep, Social, Health, Weather), tag persistence, toggle behavior to remove tags, and prompt to add context to recent widget entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Entries without tags show 'Add Context' button
Tag selector shows 5 preset options as pill buttons
Selecting a tag attaches it to entry immediately
Tapping existing tag removes it (toggle)
Prompt appears when opening app within 5 minutes of widget check-in

scenarios: Tag can be added to entry
Tag can be removed by tapping
Tag persists across app launches
validation.notes: Tag selector should use subtle pill buttons, not full-screen modal.

tasks.notes: Implementation Boundary: Tag UI and persistence only. Does NOT include custom tags (P1) or tag insights (P2). PRD refs: PR-003.

tasks.title: 7-Day Trend Chart
tasks.description: Implement 7-day trend chart using Swift Charts. Display mood values over time with daily averages, handle sparse data gracefully, add tap interaction to show exact date and value, and apply mood color scheme (blue-gray to coral gradient).
acceptanceCriteria: 7-day chart displays on Today or Insights tab
Chart shows daily average mood values
Sparse data handled with clear date range indication
Tapping data point shows exact date and average value
Chart uses mood color scheme for visual consistency

scenarios: Chart renders with 7 days of data
Chart handles fewer than 7 days gracefully
Tap interaction shows data point details
validation.notes: Use Swift Charts (iOS 16+) for native look. Chart should be readable at glance.

tasks.notes: Implementation Boundary: Chart visualization only. Does NOT include insights text or monthly view (P1). PRD refs: PR-005.

tasks.title: Basic Insights
tasks.description: Implement basic insights cards showing 'Most Common Mood' and 'Best Day of Week' when user has 7+ entries. Include encouraging message with progress indicator for users with fewer entries. Insights should recalculate automatically when data updates.
acceptanceCriteria: Most Common Mood card shows emoji and percentage (7+ entries)
Best Day card shows day of week with highest average mood
Fewer than 7 entries shows 'Check in for X more days' message
Insights recalculate automatically when new entries added
Copy is friendly and non-clinical

scenarios: Insights calculate correctly with 7+ entries
Encouraging message shows with fewer entries
Insights update after adding new entry
validation.notes: Keep calculations simple and fast. Handle sparse data carefully.

tasks.notes: Implementation Boundary: Insights calculation and cards only. Does NOT include correlation insights (P2). PRD refs: PR-006.

tasks.title: CSV Export
tasks.description: Implement CSV export functionality in Settings. Generate CSV with columns: Date, Time, Mood (1-5), Mood Emoji, Tag. Present iOS share sheet with filename 'MoodFlicker_Export_YYYY-MM-DD.csv'. Handle large datasets (1000+ entries) efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Export Data button in Settings (disabled when no entries)
CSV generated with correct columns and UTF-8 encoding
Share sheet appears with properly named file
Export confirmation shown after sharing
Handles 1000+ entries without memory issues

scenarios: CSV file generates with correct format
Share sheet presents with correct filename
Large dataset exports without crash
validation.notes: CSV uses comma delimiter with header row. Test with large dataset.

tasks.notes: Implementation Boundary: CSV export only. Does NOT include PDF export (P1). PRD refs: PR-007.
